weather_report:
Subiaco wakes to a quilt of broken cloud, the sun slipping in and out, turning the streets a muted silver rather than a hard white. Through late morning into midafternoon those gaps should widen and close like a slow-breathing lung, offering bright patches without the relentlessness of a clear sky. It won’t be a day of unbroken blue, but light will still feel lively; even with cloud the midday UV can be sneaky, so a touch of sunscreen is wise, as are sunglasses. The air reads as clean, the view crisp, with soft-edged shadows and no heavy threat of rain suggested by the pattern.

Dress in easy layers: a breathable shirt with a light top you can peel off when the sun finds you, hat at the ready. It’s a good morning for an unhurried coffee run and a lap of the shops, a brisk errand on foot, or hanging the washing to take advantage of the flashing sun. If you’re indoors, crack a window and let the tempered daylight do its gentle work.
